title: Conjecture 1.1
statement_text: A hereditary class $\mathcal{G}$ has bounded $\operatorname{{\text{\sf{tree}}-\alpha}}$ if (and only if) $\mathcal{G}$ is $(\operatorname{{\sf tw}},\omega)$-bounded.
context_text: Dallard, Milanič, and Štorgel conjectured that for hereditary graph classes, bounded tree-independence number is equivalent to (tw,ω)-boundedness. Chudnovsky and Trotignon subsequently refuted this in a strong sense for general hereditary classes. The present paper proves the positive direction holds when the hereditary class is defined by finitely many excluded induced subgraphs.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for the special case of hereditary classes defined by finitely many excluded induced subgraphs, as a corollary of the main result Theorem 1.4.
paper_title: Tree-alpha and excluding finitely many graphs

title: Conjecture 1.2
statement_text: Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a finite class of graphs. Then the class of all $\mathcal{F}$-free graphs has bounded $\operatorname{{\text{\sf{tree}}-\alpha}}$ if (and only if) it is $(\operatorname{{\sf tw}},\omega)$-bounded.
context_text: Dallard, Krnc, Kwon, Milanič, Munaro, Štorgel, and Wiederrecht posed this conjecture asserting that the (tw,ω)-boundedness characterisation of bounded tree-α holds for all hereditary classes defined by finitely many excluded induced subgraphs. This is one of the two main conjectures resolved by the present paper.
attributed_to: Dallard, Krnc, Kwon, Milanič, Munaro, Štorgel, and Wiederrecht
paper_title: Tree-alpha and excluding finitely many graphs

title: Conjecture 1.3
statement_text: Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a finite class of graphs. Then the class of all $\mathcal{F}$-free graphs has bounded $\operatorname{{\text{\sf{tree}}-\alpha}}$ if (and only if) there exist $F_{1},F_{2},F_{3}\in\mathcal{F}$ where $F_{1}$ is complete bipartite, $F_{2}$ is a subdivided multiclaw, and $F_{3}$ is the line graph of a subdivided multiclaw.
context_text: This is the explicit equivalent reformulation of Conjecture 1.2, also due to Dallard et al. [23], made precise via the observation that (tw,ω)-boundedness for $\mathcal{F}$-free classes is equivalent to excluding a complete bipartite graph, a subdivided multiclaw, and the line graph of a subdivided multiclaw. The paper proves a stronger statement replacing the line graph of a subdivided multiclaw with line graphs of all subdivisions of a wall.
attributed_to: Dallard, Krnc, Kwon, Milanič, Munaro, Štorgel, and Wiederrecht
paper_contribution: Proved by this paper via Theorem 1.4, which establishes a stronger result replacing 'line graph of a subdivided multiclaw' with 'line graphs of all subdivisions of some wall'.
paper_title: Tree-alpha and excluding finitely many graphs

title: Conjecture 1.5
statement_text: For all $a,b\in\mathbb{N}$, the class of all $K_{a,a}$-free graphs with no induced subgraph isomorphic to the $b$-vertex path has bounded $\operatorname{{\text{\sf{tree}}-\alpha}}$.
context_text: This weaker conjecture of Dallard, Krnc, Kwon, Milanič, Munaro, Štorgel, and Wiederrecht was already open for $(a,b)\in\{(2,7),(3,5)\}$ and had only recently been proved for $(a,b)=(2,6)$. It is a special case of Conjecture 1.2 obtained by taking $\mathcal{F}=\{K_{a,a}, P_b\}$.
attributed_to: Dallard, Krnc, Kwon, Milanič, Munaro, Štorgel, and Wiederrecht
paper_contribution: Settled completely as a consequence of the main result.
paper_title: Tree-alpha and excluding finitely many graphs
